data.body:
PASS-1 review (G6 â€” approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection):

AFFIRMATIONS:
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the proposed mechanism is sound and complete. Handlers `raise` (framework converts to an `isError` result) with `ToolError` reserved for intentional client-facing messages â€” the FastMCP-native convention. Verified against `src/rebar/mcp_server.py`: a named `logger` already exists (line 23) for the diagnostic-migration target, and the :892 fix the plan names is correctly specified â€” the failure-recording path logs BOTH the recorder error (with `exc_info=True`) and the original run error `exc`, so the error-reporting path can no longer mask the original failure (lines 890-904).
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: the sweep solves the named problem (broad-excepts swallowing failures, stdout pollution on the MCP-over-stdio path) rather than a proxy. Keeping the server off stdout is correctly load-bearing for JSON-RPC framing (see `main()` / `install_stderr_handler` at lines 912-918).
- APPROACH SELECTION: I generated a structurally-different alternative â€” a centralized error-handling decorator/wrapper around every tool handler instead of per-handler `raise`/`ToolError`. The plan's choice is defensibly at-least-as-good: it leans on the framework's native `isError` conversion (lower blast radius, no new abstraction, codebase-aligned with the existing `# noqa` convention). No clearly-superior alternative was missed; discarding mine. The background-thread broad-except (line 890) is correctly preserved as data-in-state rather than raised â€” a justified exception to the raise convention, not an anti-pattern.

FINDINGS:
- [ ] State a POSITIVE rationale for choosing `raise`/`ToolError` over returning structured error dicts. The plan asserts the convention ("the framework converts to an `isError` result") but does not say WHY this is the right fit â€” e.g. that returning error dicts as normal results makes failures indistinguishable from success at the protocol layer, defeating client-side error handling, whereas `raise` lets FastMCP set `isError` and `ToolError` carries clean client-facing text. criteria: [G6]; location: "What" section / AC line 1 ("MCP tool handlers raise..."); evidence: the plan names the mechanism but the only rationale is the mechanical "framework converts" â€” the fitness argument (protocol-level distinguishability of error vs. success) is absent.
